ogTitle: Frying Pan vs Fullerton Cove: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Frying Pan and Fullerton Cove.
heading: Frying Pan vs Fullerton Cove — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Frying Pan, New South Wales and Fullerton Cove, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Frying Pan has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Fullerton Cove (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Frying Pan or Fullerton Cove safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Frying Pan has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Fullerton Cove scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Frying Pan scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Fullerton Cove scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Frying Pan or Fullerton Cove?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Frying Pan has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
